title: Rich Japanese Tofu Oyakodon
description: A mustardy, fluffy, vegan-friendly tofu oyakodon with toasted caraway and sashimi-cut tofu, perfect for a date night.
instructions: Prepare rice according to package instructions and set aside.
In a bowl, whisk together tamari, mirin, rice vinegar, sugar, Japanese mustard, and 1 cup vegetable broth to create the sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat, then add toasted caraway seeds and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add tofu slices to the skillet and gently flip them once, letting them absorb flavors for 3 minutes total.
Pour the sauce into the skillet, reduce heat, and simmer for 5 minutes until lightly thickened.
In a separate small saucepan, gently heat silken tofu, stirring until it becomes slightly fluffy and warmed through.
Divide the cooked rice onto two bowls, then top each with the fluffy tofu, then pour the sauce and tofu mixture over the rice.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds, then serve hot.
